title: ActiveCampaign vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs Drip: pricing, features, automation, and ecommerce capabilities to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ActiveCampaign and Drip are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign excels as an all-in-one solution with advanced automation and CRM capabilities, while Drip is purpose-built for ecommerce businesses seeking deep store integration. Choosing between them depends on your industry, budget, and feature priorities.
choose_a_if: You need an integrated CRM platform beyond just email marketing
Budget is a primary concern and you want the lowest starting price
You require advanced lead scoring and multi-channel automation across email and SMS
choose_b_if: You run an ecommerce business and need revenue attribution and product tracking
You prefer a visual automation builder designed specifically for online stores
You want onsite campaign capabilities to engage customers on your website

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ActiveCampaign is better for businesses needing comprehensive CRM and advanced automation across multiple channels. Drip is superior for ecommerce-focused companies seeking specialized store integration and revenue tracking. ActiveCampaign has a higher overall rating (4.5 vs 4.4 on G2) and broader feature set.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Drip?
faq.a: ActiveCampaign is significantly cheaper at $15/month compared to Drip's $39/month starting price. However, Drip's features are specifically tailored for ecommerce, so the higher price may offer better value for online retailers. Total cost will depend on your subscriber count and specific feature needs.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate from Drip to ActiveCampaign. Both platforms support standard email list exports and integrations with migration tools. You may need to rebuild some automation workflows, as the platforms have different automation builders and logic, but your contact data can transfer smoothly.
